description_long: This is a vintage, red triplane with three stacked wings, reminiscent of early 20th-century fighter aircraft. The fuselage is cylindrical and tapers toward the tail, with a wooden two-blade propeller mounted at the nose. The wings are straight and feature prominent white cross insignias, and the tail section includes a single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers. The cockpit is open and situated just behind the upper wing, with a small windshield visible. The landing gear consists of two main wheels and a small tail skid, all fixed and non-retractable. The overall construction appears to be primarily metal and wood, with fabric likely covering the wings and control surfaces.
description: A red, vintage triplane with three stacked wings, white cross insignias, a wooden propeller, and an open cockpit. The airplane features fixed landing gear and a single vertical tail fin.
description_view_0: Front view showing the three stacked wings, wooden propeller, and cylindrical nose.
description_view_1: Angled side view highlighting the cross insignias on the wings and fuselage, as well as the open cockpit and fixed landing gear.
description_view_2: Top-down view displaying the full wingspan, cross insignias, and the tail's shape.
description_view_3: Opposite angled side view showing the tail section, rear fuselage, and the other side of the landing gear.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Triplane configuration with three straight, mid-mounted wings stacked vertically; no winglets or pronounced dihedral/anhedral angles.
attribute_engine_type: Single front-mounted piston engine with a two-blade wooden propeller.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Fixed taildragger landing gear with two main wheels and a tail skid.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Open, single-seat cockpit located just behind the upper wing, forward on the fuselage.
